commit_message:
ESQL: gate external-basic BWC on KEYWORD-only cap (#147245)

`MixedClusterEsqlSpecIT` was failing on
`test {csv-spec:external-basic.scoreFunction}` (#145352) and
`test {csv-spec:external-basic.topSnippetsFunction}` (#145353) with:

```
Different column type for column [title] (KEYWORD != TEXT)
expected:<KEYWORD> but was:<TEXT>
```

`externalRerankBooks` shares the same root cause though had not yet
been reported muted.

readers to map string typed-schema / `String`/`VARCHAR`/`CHAR` columns
to `KEYWORD` instead of `TEXT`, and updated `external-basic.csv-spec`
expectations accordingly. Pre-change coordinators still return `TEXT`,
so on a mixed cluster those tests fail whenever an old node coordinates
the query.

To confirm this was the sole cause (and not anything else in the
`d4f31dec6b2..HEAD` range), in a throwaway worktree at HEAD I reverted
*only* `CsvFormatReader.parseDataType` so `TEXT`/`TXT` maps back to
`DataType.TEXT`, kept every other file identical, and ran the
single-node `EsqlSpecIT.test {csv-spec:external-basic.scoreFunction}`.
It reproduced the exact same assertion, byte-for-byte. A single-node
cluster has one node = one coordinator, so this precisely models the
mixed-cluster case where the old-version node coordinates.

Adds `DATASOURCE_FILE_READERS_NO_TEXT_TYPE` to `EsqlCapabilities`, and
gates the three affected tests in `external-basic.csv-spec` with it.
Mixed clusters containing any pre-#145334 node will have
`_capabilities?capabilities=datasource_file_readers_no_text_type`
return `supported: false` (via `NodesCapabilitiesResponse.isSupported`
which is `allMatch` across nodes), so these tests `assumeTrue`-skip
rather than fail. Clusters where every node has the capability still
run them. Same pattern as #124764 (`STARTS_WITH_ENDS_WITH_LUCENE_PUSHDOWN`)
and #113022 (`spatialGeometryCollectionStats`).

Also unmutes both entries from `muted-tests.yml`.

Closes #145352
Closes #145353

[ESQL] Fix release tests (#145391)

issue_title: Any condition applied on a join/sequence/sample key, gets propagated to all rules
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.x and 9.x.

### Installed Plugins

-

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

25.4.0 Darwin Kernel Version 25.4.0

### Problem Description

The EQL optimizer's `PropagateJoinKeyConstraints` rule propagates conditions on join/sequence keys from **missing event** (`!`) filters to all other rules in a sequence. This is incorrect: while propagating key constraints across positive rules is a safe optimization, applying the same propagation to or from a missing-event rule produces wrong query semantics.

## Root cause

`PropagateJoinKeyConstraints` in
[`x-pack/plugin/eql/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/eql/optimizer/Optimizer.java` (around line 361)](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/main/x-pack/plugin/eql/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/eql/optimizer/Optimizer.java#L361)
is documented as:

> Any condition applied on a join/sequence/sample key, gets propagated to all rules.

The optimization predates the introduction of missing-event (negated) rules. Propagating key constraints across positive rules is safe because all events in the sequence must share the same key value anyway. However, the same reasoning does not hold when a negated rule carries an additional condition on the key field — that condition is local to the absent-event predicate and must not be injected into positive rules.

The `rule()` method does not call `isMissingEventFilter()` before adding a filter's constraints to the propagation set, so all constraints, regardless of whether they originate from a positive or a missing-event filter, are unconditionally broadcast to every other rule.




### Steps to Reproduce

Index a few documents and run the following EQL sequence query:

```json
GET my-index/_eql/search
{
  "query": """
    sequence by user.name
      ![authentication where event.dataset == "systemA" and user.name == "sam"]
      [authentication where event.dataset == "systemB"]
  """
}
```
The optimizer rewrites the query as:

```
sequence by user.name
  ![authentication where event.dataset == "systemA" and user.name == "sam"]
  [authentication where event.dataset == "systemB" and user.name == "sam"]
```

The condition `user.name == "sam"` from the missing-event rule is pushed into the second (positive) rule, restricting it to only `sam`'s events in `systemB`. This changes the query semantics entirely: events from other users in `systemB` are silently excluded.

The same issue occurs with `maxspan`, `join by`, and `sample` constructs whenever one of the rules is negated.


### Logs (if relevant)

_No response_

ESQL: Fix SumLongGrouping test (#145896)

Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/145438

Since https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/139973, aggs may now split their output into multiple pages.

Fix intercepted query cases that can create orphaned unique async action consumers (#145577)

commit_message:
Fix testCancelOnExpiry (#146015)

The test failed because it used a small keep_alive on submission,
leading to cases where data-node drivers were slow to start and the
query was cancelled before any driver had started. This PR modifies the
test to check for cancellation in two steps: submit with a large
keep_alive and wait until at least one data-node driver has started,
then adjust the keep_alive to a much smaller interval and expect
cancellation to happen.

Closes #145502

commit_message:
Fix flaky testContainsIterator by guaranteeing matching docs exist (#145647)

The previous setup used randomBoolean() to decide whether each possible
value contained the search term, meaning all values could end up
non-matching if every coin flip came up false. With a small
numPossibleValues (min 5), this had ~3% probability per run.

Fix by making all matchingValues guaranteed to contain the term, then
randomly mixing them with plain random strings at doc-write time so
there is always a meaningful split between matching and non-matching docs.

issue_title: ESQL: unmapped_fields="load" 500 error for index patterns usage
issue_body:
### Description

There is this query:

```
SET unmapped_fields="load";
from sample_dat* 
 | FORK 
          (where  NOT byte_length(message) < 11)
          (where true)
```

That results in a 500 error

```
                "type": "illegal_state_exception",
                "reason": "Multiple index patterns should be disabled with unmapped fields"
```

But, if I am doing

```
from sample_data
 | FORK 
          (where  NOT byte_length(message) < 11)
          (where true)
```

I get

```
FORK is not supported with unmapped_fields=\"load\"
```

The first error message being a 500 seems to indicate that we didn't expect to reach that part of code. We should have a clearer error message instead and one that shouldn't be a 500, **if indeed we do not support patterns**, or is the fact that there are multiple indices covered by a pattern?

Also, this one works `SET unmapped_fields="load"; from sample_dat* | WHERE client_ip::string != "foo"` which seems to indicate that we **do support unmapped_fields="load"** with index patterns.

---

Unrelated, or maybe it is, the following query results in

```
argument of [byte_length(event_duration)] must be [string], found value [event_duration] type [long]
```

```
SET unmapped_fields="load";
from sample_dat* 
 | FORK 
          (where  NOT byte_length(event_duration) < 11)
          (where true)
```

But it should complain about `FORK` usage, as well. Or, primarily, complain about `FORK`.

issue_title: ESQL: match_phrase on a backtick-named constant_keyword field fails
issue_body:
### Description

```
from all_types | where match_phrase(`constant_keyword-foo`, "quick data")
```

```
Found 1 problem\nline 2:2: [MatchPhrase] function cannot operate on [from all_types], which is not a field from an index mapping",
                "stack_trace": "org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.VerificationException: Found 1 problem\nline 2:2: [MatchPhrase] function cannot operate on [from all_types], which is not a field from an index mapping\r\n\tat org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.optimizer.LocalLogicalPlanOptimizer.localOptimize(LocalLogicalPlanOptimizer.java:102)
```

`constant_keyword-foo` is a `constant_keyword` field in the index.

Notice the error message that says `... cannot operate on [from all_types]` and the fact that says also "is not a field from an index mapping" (which is not true).

commit_message:
Fix race in IndexingMemoryControllerIT test (#145706)

* Fix race in IndexingMemoryControllerIT test

 IndexingMemoryControllerIT.testDeletesAloneCanTriggerRefresh now waits for the assertion to be satisfied. Synchronous post-delete `writeIndexingBuffer` can race the scheduled memory checker, and the engine’s non-blocking internal refresh can return without waiting for a refresh if one is already in flight or the read lock is not available.

Closes #145609

* Only wait 5s

commit_message:
Fix flaky binary DV tests that could generate empty document lists (#145649)

The block-wise binary doc values tests used randomIntBetween(0, ...) for
their value counts, allowing a 0 result that produces no documents. An
empty index after forceMerge has 0 leaves, causing assertEquals(1, ...)
to fail. Change lower bound to 1 in all four tests and add a precondition
assert to assertBinaryValues to catch any future callers passing an empty
list.

commit_message:
Fix flaky GCS ADC fallback test by isolating in its own JVM (#146013)

When no credentials_file is configured, GoogleCloudStorageService calls
GoogleCredentials.getApplicationDefault(), which uses DefaultCredentialsProvider
to discover credentials. If credential files are inaccessible (e.g. due to
entitlement checks returning false from File.isFile()), it falls through to
the GCE metadata server.

This test exercises that code path using a mock HttpTransport that simulates
the GCE metadata server, asserting that credentials are successfully obtained
when no explicit credentials_file is set.

Relates: #145626
